# Open CoDesign

> Open CoDesign is an open-source desktop AI design tool. The open-source alternative to Anthropic Claude Design. Bring your own API key (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, OpenRouter, SiliconFlow, Ollama, any OpenAI-compatible relay). Everything runs locally. Apache-2.0 licensed.

## Key facts

- Repository: https://github.com/OpenCoworkAI/open-codesign
- Website: https://opencoworkai.github.io/open-codesign/
- License: Apache-2.0
- Latest release: v0.1.0 (2026-04-18), installers for macOS / Windows / Linux
- Stack: Electron, React 19, Vite 6, TypeScript, Tailwind v4, better-sqlite3
- Local-first: SQLite (design history) + encrypted TOML config via Electron `safeStorage`, nothing leaves the machine
- BYOK: no API proxying, no per-token surcharges, no cloud account required, no telemetry by default
- Supported providers: Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, DeepSeek, OpenRouter, SiliconFlow, local Ollama, any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Keyless (IP-allowlisted) proxies supported.
- Install size budget: ≤ 80 MB (enforced by CI)
- Platforms: macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel), Windows (x64 + arm64), Linux (AppImage)
- Minimum Node for building from source: 22 LTS; pnpm 9.15+

## What it produces

- Interactive HTML prototypes (sandboxed iframe, inlined CSS, no external dependencies)
- Interactive JSX / React components (vendored React 18 + Babel, rendered on-device)
- PPTX slide decks via `pptxgenjs` (lazy-loaded)
- PDF one-pagers via Puppeteer-core against local Chrome (lazy-loaded)
- ZIP asset bundles (HTML + CSS + JS + assets, deterministic layout)
- Markdown exports with embedded frontmatter

## Shipped features (v0.1)

- Unified provider model with dynamic model picker — every provider exposes its real model catalogue
- One-click import from Claude Code and Codex configs (reads `~/.codex/config.toml` and `~/.claude/settings.json`)
- Keyless provider support (IP-allowlisted corporate proxies and local Ollama)
- Prompt → HTML or JSX/React component in a sandboxed iframe
- Fifteen built-in demo prompts (landing page, dashboard, pitch slide, pricing, mobile app, chat UI, event calendar, blog article, receipt/invoice, portfolio, settings panel, email, cosmic-animation, organic-loaders, case-study)
- Twelve built-in design skill modules (slide decks, dashboards, landing pages, SVG charts, glassmorphism, editorial typography, heroes, pricing, footers, chat UIs, data tables, calendars)
- Anti-AI-slop design Skill injected into every generation
- Live agent panel showing streaming tool calls
- AI-generated tunable sliders — model emits design parameters (color, spacing, font); user drags to refine
- Inline comment → AI patch loop — click any element, leave a note, model rewrites only that region
- Phone / tablet / desktop responsive preview frames
- Files panel for inspecting multi-file artifacts before export
- Instant design switching via preview-iframe pool (last five designs stay hot)
- Connection diagnostic panel — one-click test for any provider
- Light + dark themes; EN + 简体中文 UI with live toggle
- Five export formats: HTML (inlined CSS), PDF (local Chrome), PPTX, ZIP, Markdown
- Generation cancellation mid-stream
- Four-tab Settings: Models, Appearance, Storage, Advanced
- Release pipeline with signed DMG / EXE / AppImage

## Coming soon (v0.2 — v0.5)

- Cost transparency (token estimate before generation, weekly spend in toolbar)
- Version snapshots + side-by-side diff + rollback
- Three-style parallel exploration
- Codebase → design system (extract Tailwind tokens, CSS variables, W3C design tokens from a local repo)
- Web capture via Playwright (on-demand)
- Apple Developer ID notarization + Windows Authenticode code-signing
- Homebrew Cask + winget + Scoop manifests verified

## Documentation

- /quickstart : install + first generation in 90 seconds
- /architecture : technical overview, package boundaries
- /roadmap : phased plan from v0.1 → v1.0

## Differentiation vs Claude Design

Open CoDesign differs from Claude Design in the following ways:
- Open CoDesign is open source (Apache-2.0); Claude Design is closed source.
- Open CoDesign supports any AI model via BYOK; Claude Design uses Anthropic Opus only.
- Open CoDesign runs entirely on the user's machine; Claude Design processes data in the cloud.
- Open CoDesign costs only the token usage of the user's own API key; Claude Design requires a paid subscription.
- Open CoDesign is a desktop application (Electron); Claude Design is a web application.
- Open CoDesign stores all designs locally in SQLite with full version history; Claude Design has limited export and no import.
- Open CoDesign ships twelve built-in design skill modules and fifteen ready-to-edit demo prompts; Claude Design is a blank canvas.
- Open CoDesign reuses your existing Claude Code or Codex config in one click; Claude Design has no config-import path.
